description: A rigid structure surrounding something; to provide with a border. 'Kelo' names what defines boundary—the edge that honors what it contains.
sound_symbolism: The word defines edge. 'Ke' marks with clear boundary, frame established. Then 'lo' opens within, space defined for content.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'kelo' (the frame). As verb: 'mia wira kelo.' (I frame the window.) For pictures, doors, windows—any bounded opening.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Frame as art—the border that complements and honors what it contains.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Structure supports—the frame holds canvas, door, or window, enabling their function.
